title: Raita
body:
The history of raita as a side dish can be traced at least to the 12th century, when a form of it appears in the Manasollasa (1137 CE), suggesting that its roots lie in the medieval period; later evidence from the Ni‘matnāma around 1500 CE shows it more clearly as part of established courtly cuisine.

To accompany Mumbai-Style Oven Roasted Chicken Thighs with Vegetables.

Let's make a very proper cucumber raita and to fit the meal beautifully.

Use:

title: 30 Minute Meal: Mediterranean Fish & Chickpea Skillet
body:
30 Minute Meal: Mediterranean Fish & Chickpea Skillet


    Ingredients


       1 pound (500g) cod fish
       1/4 cup olive oil
       salt, to taste
       freshly cracked black pepper to taste
       1 onion, halved and thinly sliced
       4-5 garlic cloves, grated
       a handful of grape tomatoes
       2 teaspoons capers
       1 heaping teaspoon dried oregano
       10-12 Kalamata olives
       1 (15 oz can) chickpeas
       2 tablespoons finely chopped parsley
ingredient_lines_raw: 1 pound (500g) cod fish
1/4 cup olive oil
4-5 garlic cloves, grated
2 teaspoons capers
1 heaping teaspoon dried oregano
1 (15 oz can) chickpeas
2 tablespoons finely chopped parsley

title: Greek leftover rice
body:
To make Jimmy the Greek rice, you’ll need the following ingredients:

– 2 cups cooked white rice

– 1/4 cup chicken or vegetable broth

– Juice and zest of 1 lemon

– 2 tablespoons chopped fresh dill

– Salt and pepper to taste

Here’s how to make it:


In a large skillet, heat the chicken or vegetable broth over medium heat.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet, stirring to combine with the broth.
Stir in the lemon juice and zest, along with the chopped dill.
ingredient_lines_raw: – 2 cups cooked white rice
– 1/4 cup chicken or vegetable broth
– 2 tablespoons chopped fresh dill

    🥙 Greek Souvlaki Pita Night


    🍚 1️⃣ Broth Rice (Flavor Upgrade)


    Cook your rice in homemade broth instead of water.


    When it’s done:


    Fluff


    Squeeze fresh lemon


    Tiny drizzle olive oil


    Small pinch oregano (optional)


    Salt only if broth needs it


    Let it rest covered 5 minutes. It’ll get glossy and fluffy.

title: Welcome Rosemary
body:
Welcome Rosemary

Top dress rosemary with gravel — helps avoid fungus and looks cool.

Cilantro

Cilantro likes way more water then I ever imagined. No wonder my cilantro always didn't make it. It wants as much water as parsley does. You can learn a lot about a plant by growing it indoors.

title: lazy leftovers
body:
Cast iron can absolutely handle this, and adding a little liquid will give you the best of both worlds: gentle cooking without drying out, and a bit of pan sauce at the end.



🍳 Cast Iron Nuoc Cham Chicken (Semi-Hands-Off)

What to do


Preheat pan: Medium-high, add a thin film of neutral oil.
Quick sear: Lay your marinated chicken slices in a single layer. Let them get a little color (1–2 min per side).

title: Korean-Flare Basmati Rice
body:
To accompany bok choy and salmon

🍚 Korean-Flare Basmati Rice (1–2 servings)

Ingredients


½–¾ cup basmati rice (dry, uncooked)
1 cup chicken broth (for ½ cup rice) or 1½ cups broth (for ¾ cup rice)
1 tsp toasted sesame oil (plus more to drizzle)
½ tsp soy sauce (optional, adds umami + saltiness)
Pinch of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) or red pepper flakes (optional, for heat)
½ tsp garlic powder or 1 small clove fresh garlic, minced
Sprinkle of black pepper
Garnish: sliced green onion, sesame s
ingredient_lines_raw: 🍚 Korean-Flare Basmati Rice (1–2 servings)
1 cup chicken broth (for ½ cup rice) or 1½ cups broth (for ¾ cup rice)
1 tsp toasted sesame oil (plus more to drizzle)
½ tsp garlic powder or 1 small clove fresh garlic, minced

title: Valerie Bertinelli’s One-Pan Salmon with Broccoli & Cauliflower
body:
I love this recipe because it is truly a one-pan wonder. You just put the fresh salmon and crisp veggies on a baking sheet, season everything and pop it in the oven to roast. In just 20 minutes you've got a flavorful and healthy dinner and only one pan to clean!




One-Pan Salmon with Broccoli & Cauliflower

(Valerie Bertinelli)

title: Winter watering 🫖
body:
Let's welcome back cilantro 😄🌿 — the heat is over. It can live happily indoors with parsley + chives.

A simple watering plan by plant type will make winter growing easier, because there is a mix of “drink like crazy” and “sip slowly” plants.

Here’s a way to categorize the collection into watering groups:



💦 Water Lovers (frequent, keep evenly moist)

Parsley

Chives

Cilantro (new roommate!)

Basil

Lettuce / leafy greens

title: Tuna Quesadilla
body:
In the pantry 



        2 (120 g) cans tuna, drained -- solid pack tuna is best, again with the runny and flaked 
    

        1⁄4 cup mayonnaise (or less - else runny)
    

        1⁄4 cup prepared chunky salsa (or make some)
    

        6 8-inch flour tortillas
    

        3⁄4 cup shredded cheddar cheese (about 3 oz) directions 
    


    Combine tuna, mayo and salsa. 


    Spread 3 tortillas with the tuna mixture, top with cheese and cover with remaining tortillas.
ingredient_lines_raw: 2 (120 g) cans tuna, drained -- solid pack tuna is best, again with the runny and flaked
1⁄4 cup mayonnaise (or less - else runny)
1⁄4 cup prepared chunky salsa (or make some)
3⁄4 cup shredded cheddar cheese (about 3 oz) directions
Spread 3 tortillas with the tuna mixture, top with cheese and cover with remaining tortillas.

title: Jerk shrimp and fresh fruit salad
body:
I can't think of anything more inviting right now than shrimp in a cast-iron skillet on the stove top, except for maybe shrimp marinated in Jerk sauce. Yes, the Jamaican delight with scotch-bonnet peppers, usually served with chicken. 


    It started out I had some guacamole to use up, and that led to shrimp. Which led to roasted shrimp. Which made me think of the cast-iron skillet. Leftover rice. Made me think of a salad. And yes I have blueberries. And it all come together in under 20 minutes.
    Altogether magnifique!
